identity.short_name: Bounded cubic root-filter count -> cubic-root power-sum recurrence at index T

subdomain(s): discriminant criteria for quadratic integer roots and repeated roots

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Count bounded cubics with exactly one integer root among {0,1}); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Partial sum of cubic-root power sums modulo 1000) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Count bounded integer-coefficient cubics having exactly one root in {0,1} to get T, then evaluate the Newton/Vieta power-sum sequence S_n of a fixed cubic at n=T modulo 1000.
givens: Goodness: exactly one of p(0), p(1) equals 0.
alpha,beta,gamma are roots of y^3-3y+1=0; S_n=alpha^n+beta^n+gamma^n.

parameter(s).name: coefficient_box
parameter(s).purpose: finite search space enabling exact counting

parameter(s).name: forced_root_set
parameter(s).purpose: guarantees at least one small integer root and drives case split

parameter(s).name: uniqueness_of_integer_root
parameter(s).purpose: eliminates cases with two integer roots and forces the remaining quadratic factor to have no integer roots

parameter(s).name: cubic_coefficients
parameter(s).domain: monic cubic x^3 - s1 x^2 + s2 x - s3 with integer coefficients
parameter(s).purpose: determines the symmetric sums and the induced power-sum recurrence

parameter(s).name: m
parameter(s).purpose: modulus for the final reduction (here m=1000)

parameter(s).name: output_statistic
parameter(s).purpose: output is T=_{n=0}^N S_n rather than a single S_N

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: root_filter_split
invariant(s).statement: Good triples split disjointly into c=0 with 1+a+b0 and 1+a+b+c=0 with c0.

invariant(s).name: cubic_power_sum_recurrence
invariant(s).statement: S_n=3S_{n-2}-S_{n-3} for n>=3 from the cubic relation.

invariant(s).name: forced_root_case_split
invariant(s).statement: Since P(0)P(1)=0 and P has exactly one integer root, the unique integer root r is exactly one of {0,1}.

invariant(s).name: factorization_by_integer_root
invariant(s).statement: If r is a root then P(x)=(x-r)Q(x) with Q monic quadratic with integer coefficients.

invariant(s).name: quadratic_integer_root_test
invariant(s).statement: Q has an integer root iff its discriminant is a perfect square; Q has a repeated root iff its discriminant is 0.
